name: London Ambulance Service
description:
The London Ambulance Service dispatches ambulances in emergencies. 
    The key goal is to allocate an available ambulance for every call that can reach the scene within 11 minutes. 
    The entire dispatch process must not exceed a set maximum time limit.

    To make this work, the computer system (CAD) analyzes incident forms and assigns vehicles. 
    It is vital to track the exact location of moving ambulances. 
    This requires staff to follow standard routes and correctly communicate departure and destination, 
    allowing radio operators and the CAD to record data accurately.
highLevelGoals: The dispatcher aims to allocate an available ambulance for every emergency call promptly, ensuring that response times meet the 11-minute target and the entire dispatch process adheres to the maximum time limit.
The ambulance staff seeks to ensure that their exact location is tracked in real-time to facilitate efficient dispatch and response, adhering to standard routes for optimal performance.

The radio operator aims to maintain clear and accurate communication with ambulances to ensure timely updates and data recording during dispatch, contributing to the overall efficiency of the process.
The caller desires a swift and efficient response to their emergency call, ensuring that help arrives as quickly as possible, thereby enhancing the overall emergency response experience.
The patient requires prompt medical assistance during emergencies, with the expectation that ambulances arrive within the designated time frame, ensuring their health and safety.
The CAD system administrator aims to ensure the CAD system operates reliably and accurately, supporting the dispatch process effectively and maintaining compliance with operational standards.
lowLevelGoals: The dispatcher queries the system to get the current status of all ambulances, including their availability and location.
The dispatcher applies filters to the list of available ambulances to identify those closest to the emergency site.
The dispatcher selects an ambulance from the filtered list of available units and assigns it to the incoming emergency call.
The dispatcher marks the allocated ambulance as on its way to the emergency location in the CAD system.
The system continuously updates the location of the allocated ambulance as it moves towards the emergency site, providing the dispatcher with live updates.
The ambulance staff transmits their current GPS coordinates back to the CAD system for tracking purposes.
The ambulance staff confirms receipt of location tracking instructions and updates their status accordingly.
The radio operator relays information about the ambulance's current status and location to the dispatcher.
The radio operator records the emergency call details and the assigned ambulance information in the CAD system for future reference.
The caller contacts the emergency services by making a phone call to the designated emergency number.
The caller communicates critical information regarding the emergency to the dispatcher, including location and nature of the incident.
The patient or caller acknowledges the arrival of the ambulance and provides any additional information needed for medical assistance.
The CAD system administrator conducts regular system checks to verify that all components are functioning correctly and efficiently.
The CAD system administrator installs updates and patches to the CAD system to improve functionality and security.
The system calculates and displays the optimal route for the ambulance to follow, considering real-time traffic data.
The ambulance staff alerts the dispatcher if they are unable to follow the recommended route due to unforeseen circumstances.
